claim: A wave shall land as a whole with a green T2 (go test ./...) run at its boundary before the next wave starts.
why: Reworded 2026-07-12 to describe the current implementation. T1 alone (see R-t1-gate-is-default) only checks the targeted enforcer subset per move; without a mandatory full-suite gate at the wave boundary, cross-cutting regressions between moves in the same wave could slip through undetected. `hotam land --batch` (cmd/hotam/land.go -> proposal.ApplyBatch) lands a whole directory of proposals atomically (all-or-nothing) with one apply -> gen-spec -> all-violations pass, and spec/CLAUDE.md's Mediation loop step 6 LAND names `go test ./...` as MANDATORY at wave/commit boundaries. Enforcement is honestly STRUCTURAL: what-now/land make the T2 boundary visible and addressable but no single check_* fires automatically if a steward skips a wave-boundary `go test ./...` by hand outside the tool (the gate_status / land-log.jsonl tier trace that would have made this auditable is not yet implemented -- see R-land-leaves-trace).
